# core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions { #ammonyte.core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions }

```python
core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions(
    series,
    jump_times,
    jump_values,
    method,
    method_args=None,
    label=None,
    statistics=None,
)
```

Container for deterministic transition detection results

Stores results from tipping point detection algorithms with methods for analysis and visualization.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**series**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[ammonyte](`ammonyte`).[Series](`ammonyte.Series`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Original time series object

<code>[**jump_times**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[array](`array`) - [like](`like`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Array of detected transition times

<code>[**jump_values**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[array](`array`) - [like](`like`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Array of transition directions (+1 upward, -1 downward)

<code>[**method**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Name of detection method used

<code>[**method_args**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Dictionary of method parameters

<code>[**label**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Label for the results

<code>[**d_statistics**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[array](`array`) - [like](`like`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Array of KS D-statistics corresponding to each transition

<code>[**p_values**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [([array](`array`) - [like](`like`), [optional](`optional`))]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Array of p-values corresponding to each transition

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Basic transition detection:

.. jupyter-execute::

    import os, ammonyte as amt
    ngrip = amt.Series.from_csv(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(amt.__file__), 'data', 'NGRIP.csv'))
    transitions = ngrip.kstest(w_min=0.12, w_max=2.5, n_w=15, d_c=0.77, n_c=3, s_c=2, x_c=0.8)
    print(transitions)
    
Plot the results:

.. jupyter-execute::

    transitions.plot()
    
Access transition data:

.. jupyter-execute::

    print(f"Number of transitions: {len(transitions.jump_times)}")
    print(f"First transition: {transitions.jump_times[0]:.2f}")

## See Also {.doc-section .doc-section-see-also}

Series.kstest : Detect transitions

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [copy](#ammonyte.core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions.copy) | Create a copy of the DeterministicTransitions object. |
| [plot](#ammonyte.core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions.plot) | Plot the time series with detected transitions. |
| [to_csv](#ammonyte.core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions.to_csv) | Export detected transitions to CSV file |

### copy { #ammonyte.core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions.copy }

```python
core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions.copy()
```

Create a copy of the DeterministicTransitions object.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[DeterministicTransitions](`ammonyte.core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A deep copy of the current object.

### plot { #ammonyte.core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions.plot }

```python
core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions.plot(
    figsize=(12, 8),
    ylabel=None,
    xlabel=None,
    title=None,
    upward_color='red',
    downward_color='blue',
    transition_color=None,
    show_transitions='both',
    ax=None,
    title_fontsize=14,
    label_fontsize=12,
    tick_fontsize=10,
    legend_fontsize=10,
    show_legend=True,
    legend_labels=None,
    legend_loc='best',
    **kwargs,
)
```

Plot the time series with detected transitions.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**figsize**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tuple](`tuple`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [(12, 8)]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Figure size (width, height) in inches. Default is (12, 8).

<code>[**ylabel**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Y-axis label. If None, uses series metadata.

<code>[**xlabel**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   X-axis label. If None, uses series metadata.

<code>[**title**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Plot title. If None, auto-generated.

<code>[**upward_color**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [\'red\']{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Color for upward transition markers. Default is 'red'. Used when show_transitions is 'both' or 'upward'.

<code>[**downward_color**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [\'blue\']{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Color for downward transition markers. Default is 'blue'. Used when show_transitions is 'both' or 'downward'.

<code>[**transition_color**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Color for all transitions when show_transitions='all'. Default is None. If None, falls back to upward_color. Recommended to use this parameter when show_transitions='all' for clarity.

<code>[**show_transitions**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [\'both\']{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Which transitions to show: 'all', 'both', 'upward', or 'downward'. Default is 'both'. - 'all': Show all transitions in one color without direction distinction (use transition_color) - 'both': Show upward and downward transitions in different colors (use upward_color/downward_color) - 'upward': Show only upward transitions (use upward_color) - 'downward': Show only downward transitions (use downward_color)

<code>[**ax**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[matplotlib](`matplotlib`).[axes](`matplotlib.axes`).[Axes](`matplotlib.axes.Axes`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Axes object to plot on. If None, creates new figure.

<code>[**title_fontsize**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [14]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Font size for plot title. Default is 14.

<code>[**label_fontsize**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [12]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Font size for axis labels. Default is 12.

<code>[**tick_fontsize**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [10]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Font size for tick labels (numbers on axes). Default is 10.

<code>[**legend_fontsize**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [10]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Font size for legend. Default is 10.

<code>[**show_legend**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[bool](`bool`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Whether to display legend. Default is True.

<code>[**legend_labels**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [list of str]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Custom legend labels. If None, uses auto-generated labels. Provide as list: ['label1'] for single transition type, or ['label1', 'label2'] for both.

<code>[**legend_loc**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [\'best\']{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Legend location. Default is 'best'. Options: 'upper right', 'upper left', 'lower left', 'lower right', 'right', 'center left', 'center right', 'lower center', 'upper center', 'center', 'best'.

<code>[****kwargs**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [{}]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Additional arguments passed to matplotlib plot functions.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[**fig**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[matplotlib](`matplotlib`).[figure](`matplotlib.figure`).[Figure](`matplotlib.figure.Figure`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The figure object.

<code>[**ax**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[matplotlib](`matplotlib`).[axes](`matplotlib.axes`).[Axes](`matplotlib.axes.Axes`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The axes object.

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> # Basic plot
>>> result.plot()
>>>
>>> # Custom colors and size
>>> result.plot(figsize=(15, 10), upward_color='green', downward_color='purple')
>>>
>>> # Show only upward transitions
>>> result.plot(show_transitions='upward', upward_color='red')
>>>
>>> # Show all transitions without direction distinction (single color, single legend)
>>> result.plot(show_transitions='all', transition_color='blue')
>>>
>>> # Custom font sizes
>>> result.plot(title_fontsize=18, label_fontsize=16, tick_fontsize=14, legend_fontsize=14)
>>>
>>> # Hide legend
>>> result.plot(show_legend=False)
>>>
>>> # Custom legend label with 'all' mode
>>> result.plot(show_transitions='all', transition_color='purple',
...             legend_labels=['Detected Transitions'])
>>>
>>> # Custom legend location and color
>>> result.plot(show_transitions='all', transition_color='#1f77b4',
...             legend_loc='upper right')
```

### to_csv { #ammonyte.core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions.to_csv }

```python
core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions.to_csv(path=None, **kwargs)
```

Export detected transitions to CSV file

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Output file path. If None, uses default naming based on method.

<code>[****kwargs**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [{}]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Additional arguments passed to pandas.to_csv()

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> transitions = ngrip.kstest(w_min=0.12, w_max=2.5)
>>> transitions.to_csv('my_transitions.csv')
```
